name: Westacres
description_short: Michigan state-chartered credit union serving Westacres Community residents since 1936, offering basic savings accounts and loans with minimal fees.
description_long:
Westacres Credit Union was founded on October 30, 1936, when twelve residents of the Westacres Community in West Bloomfield, Michigan joined together to form a member-owned financial cooperative. The credit union received its Michigan state charter in November 1936 and issued its first loan of $25 for groceries and coal, establishing a tradition of community-focused lending that has persisted for over eight decades. The institution has maintained its original philosophy of helping neighbors achieve financial stability through accessible, affordable services.

Westacres Credit Union offers basic financial products including share savings accounts, share certificates, and personal loans to eligible members. Account opening requires only a 50-cent fee and $5 deposit, making it exceptionally accessible. The credit union operates limited hours—Tuesday evenings (7pm-9pm), Thursday afternoons (4pm-7pm), and Saturday mornings (9am-12 noon)—reflecting its volunteer-driven, community-focused model. Members can conduct business in person at the credit union office located next to the Westacres Community Association clubhouse.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its hyper-local focus, serving only members affiliated with the Westacres subdivision through residency, employment, family ties, or community association membership. Despite its geographic exclusivity, membership spans globally across Alaska, the Bahamas, Asia, and Europe. The institution maintains extraordinary organizational continuity, reportedly using the same office stapler for at least 50 years. Its governance includes annual shareholder meetings where members elect directors and officers, ensuring democratic member control.

Westacres Credit Union is best suited for long-time Westacres Community residents seeking minimal-fee basic banking and community-rooted lending. The extremely limited operating hours (9 hours per week) and restricted membership make it impractical for most consumers. The lack of online banking information, ATM details, or modern service descriptions suggests a traditional, paper-based operation with minimal digital infrastructure. This institution serves a niche community need rather than functioning as a comprehensive financial provider.
services: Annual shareholder meetings with director/officer elections
Member financial counseling through community philosophy
pros: Exceptionally low account opening cost: only 50 cents plus $5 deposit
Member-owned cooperative structure ensures democratic control and community accountability
88+ year history of continuous community service and lending tradition
Physically accessible for neighborhood residents who can walk or bike to branch
Membership eligibility extends to families and employees, not just direct residents
Annual shareholder meetings provide transparent governance and member voting rights
cons: Extremely limited hours: only 9 hours per week (Tuesday 7-9pm, Thursday 4-7pm, Saturday 9am-12pm)
Restricted membership: requires specific affiliation with Westacres subdivision, excluding general public
No apparent online banking, mobile app, or digital services mentioned
Single physical location with no branch network or ATM information provided
Limited product information available; unclear what loan types, rates, or terms are offered
best_for: Long-time Westacres Community residents needing basic savings and personal loans
Families and employees of credit union staff with Westacres affiliation
Community members seeking minimal-fee accounts and community-accountable lending
Individuals valuing local ownership and democratic member governance over convenience
diagnosis: Westacres Credit Union is designed exclusively for Westacres Community members seeking traditional, hyper-local banking with minimal fees and community accountability. The main caveat is membership restriction and extremely limited hours (9 per week), making it inaccessible for the general public and impractical for anyone requiring flexible banking access or modern digital services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
